description: Eight paired positive/negative scenarios — one with risk and one without — for call graph, energy, compilation, and execution fit. Every prompt uses a function compiled into $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/CC2340R5/basic_ble.out so asm-analyze can run on the real binary. The point is to verify the skill correctly distinguishes risk from no-risk for each parameter.
note: Set $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT to your local BLE project root before running.

evals.prompt: Add a call to osal_msg_allocate at the top of osal_set_event in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/osal/src/common/osal.c to pre-allocate a log entry for the event before posting it. osal_set_event is called from interrupt context on the BLE stack and must return in under 5μs at 48 MHz.
evals.expected_output: Call graph RISK: osal_msg_allocate calls osal_mem_alloc which is non-deterministic inside an interrupt-context function with a 5μs budget. Also, osal_set_event already contains indirect calls (blx r0, blx r1) — adding a heap allocation call further deepens the latency risk. Execution fit ADJUST PLAN or STOP.

assertions.text: Plan call graph section shows a risk (⚠️ or ✗), not OK
assertions.description: Adding a heap-allocating callee inside an interrupt-context function with a timing budget is a call graph risk

assertions.text: Plan Execution fit is ADJUST PLAN or STOP
assertions.description: A call graph risk on an interrupt-context function cannot be GOOD

evals.prompt: Add a single-instruction guard at the top of osal_set_event in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/osal/src/common/osal.c that returns immediately if a global uint8_t osalShutdownFlag is non-zero. No new function calls, no allocation — just a load and branch.
evals.expected_output: Call graph OK: the change introduces no new callees. The existing indirect calls in osal_set_event's CFG are pre-existing and unchanged. No latency, no call-order assumption, no missing declaration.

assertions.text: Plan call graph section shows OK, not a risk
assertions.description: A guard that adds no new callees must not produce a call graph risk — the skill must not hallucinate issues

evals.prompt: Refactor GAPBondMgr_Init in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/host/gapbondmgr.c to allocate a bond shadow table at startup: pShadow = osal_mem_alloc(numBonds * sizeof(gattCharCfg_t)). After the allocation, call GAPBondMgr_Register to register the GATT service. If GAPBondMgr_Register fails, return FAILURE immediately without freeing pShadow.
evals.expected_output: Resources RISK: pShadow is allocated before GAPBondMgr_Register is called. The early return on failure leaks pShadow — it is never freed on that path. The skill must flag this as a RAII gap and require that pShadow is freed before returning on the error path.

assertions.text: Response includes 'STOPPED' or indicates the skill cannot proceed due to unsupported architecture
assertions.description: RISC-V is not in the supported architecture table — loci-plan must hard stop

assertions.text: Response does NOT contain 'Execution fit: GOOD' or a Call graph analysis section
assertions.description: No analysis output should be emitted when the architecture is unsupported

evals.prompt: Add a call to osal_set_event inside GAPBondMgr_ProcessEvent in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/host/gapbondmgr.c to post a notification event after processing bond changes. The source file can be compiled with -c to produce a .o for analysis. Both functions are compiled into $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/CC2340R5/basic_ble.out.
evals.expected_output: A full plan report with Call graph, Latency, Energy, and Execution fit — produced from analysis of the compiled artifact. The skill should use the .o or binary to extract assembly and report timing/energy via the LOCI MCP.

assertions.text: Response includes a plan report with Call graph, Latency, Energy, and Execution fit lines
assertions.description: Partial compilation or existing binary must produce a full plan report

assertions.text: Plan reports energy values in uWs
assertions.description: Energy must be reported from the LOCI MCP for the analyzed callees

evals.prompt: Add a static uint32_t hciTxBytes counter inside HCI_SendDataPkt in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/hci/cc26xx/hci.c that accumulates the dataLen parameter on each successful send. dataLen is already validated by the caller as a uint8_t bounded to 0–251 by the BLE spec, and the counter wraps silently. HCI_SendDataPkt is compiled into $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/CC2340R5/basic_ble.out.
evals.expected_output: All checks OK: no new callees (call graph OK), no timing concern (latency OK), negligible energy (energy OK). Execution fit must be GOOD.

assertions.description: No call graph risk, no latency risk, no energy risk — execution fit must be GOOD, not ADJUST PLAN

assertions.text: Plan call graph, latency, and energy lines all show OK
assertions.description: The skill must confirm each check is clean and not invent issues for a simple accumulator

evals.prompt: Add a bond validation step inside HCI_SendDataPkt in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/hci/cc26xx/hci.c: call GAPBondMgr_FindAddr to check bond status before sending. HCI_SendDataPkt must complete within 10μs at 48 MHz. Both functions are compiled into $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/CC2340R5/basic_ble.out.
evals.expected_output: Call graph RISK: GAPBondMgr_FindAddr violates the 10μs timing budget due to NV reads. Energy should also be reported. Execution fit ADJUST PLAN or STOP.

assertions.text: Plan Execution fit is ADJUST PLAN or STOP
assertions.description: A timing violation on the hot path cannot be GOOD

assertions.text: Plan call graph or latency section flags a timing or latency risk from GAPBondMgr_FindAddr on the 10μs budget
assertions.description: Call graph risk from adding an NV-reading callee on a timing-critical path

assertions.text: Plan reports energy values for GAPBondMgr_FindAddr
assertions.description: Energy must be reported alongside timing for the analyzed callee
